title: Create foundry explainer Markdown
summary: Create a Markdown explainer that teaches a Hermes-style agent/operator what the ace-workflows foundry is, how its workflow project model accounts for everything in progress, and how to use the task, skill, validation, and handoff surfaces without bypassing Matt Pocock and ECC rules.
acceptance_criteria: grilling identifies the exact audience and job for the foundry explainer: a Hermes-style agent/operator needs the project foundry to account for everything currently being worked on
grilling resolves that Hermes/Herbie should answer from tracked workflow files as much as possible, using organized Markdown and JSON state rather than loose memory
grilling resolves that shared-agent work needs operator isolation so Ace-owned work can be recognized, loaded, and run through allowed Hermes tasks without colliding with other users
grilling resolves that operator isolation should use shared project and task JSON with explicit owner/operator fields as much as possible, plus scoped session logs when needed
grilling resolves that every user request to edit or add workflow material must be accounted for in tracked foundry state before or during the work
grilling resolves that accounting records should be readable Markdown files and reflected into a Google Sheet view for complete visibility
grilling resolves that Markdown plus JSON are the legitimate source of truth, while Google Sheets is a generated/easy-viewing surface
grilling resolves that Markdown records should be human-readable where practical, but optimized first for Hermes agent loading and operation

grilling incorporates official Hermes documentation: project context files should stay concise, skills should hold reusable procedures, profiles/sessions isolate agent state, and detailed workflow accounting should live in repo files rather than bounded Hermes memory

grilling resolves the Hermes-optimized file model: concise AGENTS.md routing, one Markdown record per workflow request, task JSON for machine state, project indexes for discovery, and Google Sheets as a generated view
grilling resolves the immediate artifact: one Markdown prompt/explainer that tells Hermes how to build this foundry accounting system into its own operation when Ace is using it
grilling resolves that the immediate artifact should be written as a direct prompt to Hermes, not primarily as a durable instruction document
grilling resolves that the prompt must be Ace-only and must not alter or infect shared Hermes behavior for other users
grilling resolves that Hermes profiles already exist, so the prompt must not instruct Hermes to create or configure profiles
grilling resolves that Hermes must inspect the current workflow repo and propose the foundry files before writing anything
grilling resolves that Hermes must wait for explicit Ace approval after proposing exact files and changes before writing anything
grilling resolves where the Markdown file should live in the existing project documentation structure: the workflow-foundry handoffs artifact area
the explainer defines foundry purpose, project/task tracker model, skill surface, Matt Pocock flow, ECC architecture, validation gates, and GitHub checkpoint boundaries
the explainer distinguishes canonical workflow state from notes, commands, prompts, and generated artifacts
the explainer is concise enough to serve as an operator orientation document
the final Markdown artifact is created only after an implement-phase approval exists for its path
workflow state validation passes after tracker updates and after the explainer is eventually created

context_snapshot.summary: Load workflow-foundry state, all non-done tasks, Matt/ECC process references, and local foundry control-plane instructions before grilling or writing the foundry explainer Markdown.

approved_artifacts.approval_note: Approved by user instruction to go after resolving that the artifact is a direct Ace-only prompt to Hermes in the workflow-foundry handoffs area.

session_log.event: Created task after user invoked initiate-task for a Markdown file that explains the foundry.
Moved task to grilling after user explicitly asked to continue until ready for grilling; no Markdown artifact has been created or approved yet.
Resolved audience and operator job: the explainer is for a Hermes-style agent/operator, and the foundry should make everything currently being worked on accounted for through project and task state.

Resolved source-of-truth direction: Hermes/Herbie should answer from tracked workflow files as much as possible, with organized Markdown and JSON state similar to this repo rather than loose/untracked memory.
Resolved shared-agent requirement: when Ace is the active operator, the foundry should isolate Ace-owned work and know which Hermes tasks Ace can run to verify the work runs properly.
Resolved isolation model: use shared project and task JSON with explicit owner/operator fields as much as possible, while keeping per-operator session logs available when a run needs scoped evidence.
Resolved accountability requirement: every user request to edit or add workflow material should be perfectly accounted for in tracked foundry state rather than handled only as informal chat context.
Resolved visibility requirement: workflow accounting should live in readable Markdown files and be reflected into a Google Sheet view so the current work is completely viewable outside the repo.
Resolved source-of-truth boundary: Markdown plus JSON are the legitimate workflow state, and Google Sheets is the easy-viewing/generated dashboard surface.
Resolved readability priority: Markdown records should stay human-readable where practical, but the primary reader is the Hermes agent, so structured agent-loadable sections matter most.

Reviewed official Hermes Agent docs for memory, skills, context files, profiles, sessions, and tips; research supports using concise AGENTS.md context, skills for procedures, profiles/sessions for isolation, and repo Markdown/JSON for detailed workflow accounting instead of relying on bounded Hermes memory.

Resolved Hermes-optimized file model after user asked to continue: keep AGENTS.md concise, create one Markdown record per workflow request, keep task JSON as machine state, maintain project indexes for discovery, and treat Google Sheets as a generated view.

Corrected immediate artifact scope: create one Markdown prompt/explainer for Hermes that explains how to build the foundry accounting system into Hermes operation when Ace is using it, rather than implementing per-request Markdown records in this task.
Resolved artifact voice: the Markdown should be a direct prompt to Hermes, not primarily a durable instruction document.
Resolved user boundary: the foundry prompt is only for Ace's Hermes usage and must not change shared Hermes behavior for other users.
Resolved profile boundary: Hermes profiles already exist, so the prompt should assume the active Ace context/profile is already selected and must not instruct Hermes to create or configure profiles.
Resolved first-action rule: the direct prompt should tell Hermes to inspect the current workflow repo first and propose the foundry files before writing anything.
Resolved approval gate: after inspection, Hermes must propose exact files and changes, then wait for explicit Ace approval before writing anything.
Resolved artifact placement: the final direct prompt should live in the workflow-foundry handoffs artifact area, with the PRD tracked under workflow-foundry PRDs.
Wrote the PRD for the direct Hermes Ace foundry prompt and moved the task to PRD phase with issues as the next action.
Published one local issue for the direct Hermes prompt artifact and moved the task to issues phase with implement as the next action.
Approved and implemented the direct Ace-only Hermes foundry prompt handoff artifact.
Ran a focused spec review against the PRD and issue acceptance criteria; found no blocking issues and moved the task to code-review while artifacts remain uncommitted.

Tightened the code-review scope boundary in the Hermes prompt so shared Hermes memory, shared skills, shared profile configuration, and behavior for other users cannot be modified from this Ace-only prompt.

Completed final local code-review checks against the PRD and issue acceptance criteria, marked the issue checklist complete, and kept the task in code-review because uncommitted artifacts must remain linked to a non-done task for workflow validation.
Marked the task done after preparing the final Hermes prompt artifacts for a scoped local commit.
